title: London Beavers Just Fixed a Tube Station Engineers Couldn't
script: Beavers beat London's flood engineers! For years, the Greenford Underground station in Ealing kept flooding. Engineers drew up plans for an artificial reservoir — expensive, slow, complicated. Then conservationists proposed something wild: beavers. The Eurasian beaver went extinct in Britain 400 years ago, hunted out for fur and castoreum. In 2023, a family of five was reintroduced to 24-acre Paradise Fields right next to the station. They got to work. They built dams, carved channels, transformed the meadow into a natural wetland — all without permits, meetings, or budget approvals. Within weeks, the flooding stopped. The colony has grown to eight beavers, and Greenford hasn't flooded since. Engineers spent years planning a reservoir. The beavers built one in weeks. So — how many other London stations need a beaver?
